seed_problem: Emit a full machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence/no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).
mutation: composite: skin the job-shop as a warehouse robotics bay-scheduling instance and (input) relax durations to a heavy-tailed jam distribution at large scale, (output) require an explicit per-operation start-time schedule scored by the serial/makespan ratio 0.1*T/F, so optimal scheduling is NP-hard and dispatch rules diverge on the jam-induced idle gaps.
title: Warehouse Robot Bay Scheduling (Job-Shop Makespan)
strategies: fully-serialized baseline: run every operation one at a time (makespan = sum of all durations, score 0.1)
single-rule list scheduling: dynamic dispatch committing the shortest-processing-time ready head, placing each op at max(job-ready, bay-free)
multi-rule best-of dispatch: try SPT / LPT / most-work-remaining / earliest-completion plus randomized restarts and keep the shortest makespan
local search: seed with a dispatch schedule then swap/critical-path reorder operations on the bottleneck bay to shave the makespan
